content:
The simplest way for a 100% transfer is to copy the entire profile. Here are steps for that:

Here is the link for moving a profile in Windows when installed in default location: 

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/moving-thunderbird-data-to-a-new-computer

here is the info in brief steps:

  1. ensure thunderbird is NOT running on old machine
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media
  5. ensure thunderbird is installed on second computer and NOT running
  6. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  7. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder , copy&paste the exported Thunderbird folder there. This may prompt to overwrite an existing folder of same name. allow it
  8. start thunderbird and all should be there

If you have already setup thunderbird and accounts, you can still get the Local Folders. If you're comfortable working with windows file explorer do this:

- on old hard drive, locate the old profile at the users\<yourid>\appdata\roaming\thunderbird\profiles folder. the profiles.ini file at appdata\roaming\thunderbird will point to the proper profile - open that profile and click to the Mail\Local Folders folder and copy everything there - then, on new computer, paste it all in the Mail\Local Folders folder of the active profile - Do this when thundebird NOT running.

content:
Timeout issues are always a mess. Many are directly caused by antivirus software. It takes them so long to do nothing useful that the server just gives up and drops the connection.

So, if stopping any outgoing email scanning does not stop the rot, about the only way to get to the bottom of this sending issue is to actually log the connection to the server and see exactly what is taking to the time to result in a timeout.

So to that end. use the Config Editor to change the hidden preference mailnews.smtp.loglevel, set it to All

Now when you are ready to send your email open the error console. (Ctrl+Shift+J) and use the clear/trash button to remove everything that is shown there already.

Now click send Review the messages that appear in the console immediately before the time out. Note you can right click in the console and select copy all and then go to pastebin and make a paste of the log that David and I can look at after you post a link to the paste.

Extra note: You can manage the paste if you have an account with pastebin and are logged in. Anonymous pastes stay available forever basically for anyone with the link.

If the error are about reading a file or sending the file to the server look for antivirus issues, either scanning in the temp folder or scanning the outgoing mail (it is truly amazing how repetitive this software is looking for something it has already said it can not find (it certifies nothing found when it scans your system) but write the email to the temp file ready to send. It scans. convert any binary attachment to mime as must happen and it scans it again in memory write the modified content to temp and it scans it again send the file to the server and you guessed it, another scan.

"Insanity is doing the same thing over and over and expecting a different result." is attributed to many sources but possibly belongs to Narcotics Anonymous. That does not exclude the similarity of the actions being taken by antivirus products. Personally I think the repetition is insane. Oddly enough I am not a fan of email scanning, either incoming or outgoing because incoming with Thunderbird the risks are almost non existent and outgoing you can't find what you do not know about.

content:
AI:

Pokud v Thunderbirdu na počítači nejdou přikládat soubory k e-mailům, i když máš nejnovější verzi, může být problém v několika věcech. Zkus postupně: 1. **Restart Thunderbirdu i počítače.** Někdy pomůže jednoduchý restart aplikace nebo systému. 2. **Vyzkoušej „Nouzový režim“ (Troubleshoot Mode):** * Klikni na ☰ (menu) → **Nápověda** → **Režim řešení problémů…** * Potvrď „Restartovat“. Pokud tam přílohy fungují, vadí některé **rozšíření** nebo **motiv** – zkus je postupně vypnout. 3. **Zkontroluj antivir/firewall.** Některé antiviry blokují přístup Thunderbirdu k souborům (např. kvůli kontrole příloh). Zkus dočasně vypnout kontrolu e-mailů nebo přidat Thunderbird do výjimek. 4. **Ověř cestu k souboru.** Přílohy z „nestandardních“ míst (např. síťové disky, OneDrive offline, šifrované složky) někdy Thunderbird nepřipojí. Zkus přetáhnout soubor přímo z Plochy. 5. **Zkontroluj velikost přílohy.** E-mailové servery často omezují velikost (např. 20–25 MB). Pokud je soubor větší, použij odkaz na úložiště (např. Google Drive). 6. **Zkus nový profil Thunderbirdu.** Někdy pomůže vytvořit nový čistý profil: * Zavři Thunderbird. * Spusť `thunderbird.exe -p` * Klikni na **Vytvořit profil** a přidej svůj účet. * Otestuj, jestli přílohy jdou připojit.
